title: clinopodium serpyllifolium subsp. barbatum
intro: Clinopodium serpyllifolium subsp. barbatum, also known as creeping wild thyme or creeping savory, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of this plant include relief from digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations, as well as its potential to reduce stress and improve sleep quality. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, making it a versatile and valuable addition to natural medicine and wellness practices.
